description:
The crypto-policies package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install crypto-policies
rationale:
Centralized cryptographic policies simplify applying secure ciphers across an operating system and
the applications that run on that operating system. Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms
undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data.
ocil_clause: the crypto-policies package is not installed
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the crypto-policies package is installed: 
$ rpm -q crypto-policies
fixtext:
The crypto-policies package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install crypto-policies
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the crypto-policies package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the crypto-policies package installed.
stig.vuldiscussion: Centralized cryptographic policies simplify applying secure ciphers across an operating system and the applications that run on that operating system. Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of using encryption to protect data.
stig.checktext:
Verify that the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 crypto-policies package is installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed crypto-policies

Example output:

crypto-policies.noarch          20240828-2.git626aa59.el9_5

If the crypto-policies package is not installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Install the crypto-policies package (if the package is not already installed) with the following command:

$ sudo dnf -y install crypto-policies

title: Install crypto-policies package
